stories.title: Red Sox's Eduardo Rivera: Drawing another start
stories.summary: Red Sox's Eduardo Rivera will start the second game of Wednesday's doubleheader against the Orioles. Rivera has allowed three runs with a 9:4 K:BB over 9.1 frames this season. He's unlikely to offer much length Wednesday, having not lasted more than 3.2 innings in either of his previous starts. The Red Sox had considered Ranger Suarez for the start, but will hold Suarez back for now.

stories.title: Montana state Sen. Jason Ellsworth found guilty in official misconduct trial
stories.summary: Montana state Sen. Jason Ellsworth has been found guilty of official misconduct in a trial in Helena. A jury convicted Ellsworth of circumventing the state's contracting process to steer over $100,000 in work to a close associate. He faces up to six months in jail and a $500 fine. Sentencing is set for September 10, where Judge Chris Abbott will determine Ellsworth's punishment.

stories.title: Inter Miami - Chicago Fire [RELACJA NA ŻYWO]
stories.summary: Robert Lewandowski makes his debut in the MLS for Chicago Fire against Inter Miami on Thursday, July 23, at 1:30 am. The match will be streamed online for free on the websites of three Polish bookmakers. This is Lewandowski's first game in his new team after transferring from a previous club.

stories.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
stories.summary: Rising temperatures in Africa's hottest regions are taking a toll on camel populations, with herders reporting increased heat-related deaths and suffering among the animals. In the Afar region of north-eastern Ethiopia, camel herder Ali Umer has lost eight camel calves in the past month due to extreme heat. The region is one of the driest places on Earth, where temperatures are becoming increasingly unbearable for both humans and animals. As temperatures continue to rise, camel herders are struggling to cope with the changing climate.

stories.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
stories.summary: New research suggests that most sewage spills in England may be illegal, with 86% of raw sewage spilling hours occurring without heavy rain. This analysis, based on data from over 350 storm overflow outlets, implies that water companies may be exceeding permitted limits. The study's findings have significant implications for England's nine water companies, which serve over 30 million people. The water companies will now face scrutiny over their compliance with environmental regulations.

stories.title: Power Rankings: Son Heung-Min & LAFC get El Tráfico boost
stories.summary: Nashville SC is on pace to break the league record for regular season points with 81, after earning 19 wins with 19 games remaining. The team secured a 1-0 win over Atlanta United, with Shakur Mohammed scoring the winning goal in the second half. Nashville's ability to win despite struggling performances has been a key factor in their success. The team will look to continue their winning streak in their next match against Cincinnati on July 22.

stories.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
stories.summary: The National Health Service in England has made the life-changing drug fampridine available to thousands of multiple sclerosis patients, potentially benefiting up to 5,000 people each year. The medication, known as a "signal booster," helps nerve signals to improve walking speed and distance for some patients. This decision comes after clinical trials showed around four in 10 people taking the drug experienced improvements. Patients can now access this treatment alongside existing NHS support, including physiotherapy and walking aids.

stories.title: Indian police cracked down on 'cockroach' protesters. They went home and made memes about it
stories.summary: Indian police cracked down on tens of thousands of protesters in Delhi on Monday, using tear gas and batons to disperse the crowd. The protesters, supporters of the Cockroach Janta Party, were demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over exam paper leaks and recruitment scandals. The crackdown resulted in no reported fatalities, but the incident has sparked widespread criticism. As the protesters return home, they are taking to social media to mock the police response with humorous memes.

stories.title: Strike 2: An old-school way to fix what’s wrong with baseball
stories.summary: Major League Baseball has implemented rule changes to boost offense, including banning the shift and limiting mid-inning pitching changes. As a result, fewer than a dozen players currently hit .300 or above. The changes aim to counter the dominance of pitchers, who now face hitters only once per night. The next test of these changes will come in the upcoming regular season.

stories.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
stories.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK's major airports have increased by a third on average since last summer, with two London airports, Gatwick and Stansted, charging the highest fees at £10. This rise comes as Gatwick aims to encourage more passengers to arrive by trains and buses. The fees are part of airports' business models and climate change objectives. The airport's goal to reduce reliance on cars is being tested as it pursues a second runway.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: US Secretary of Energy Chris Wright announced a landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, allowing the country to enrich uranium on its soil at a US-built facility. The deal, worth billions of dollars, will be built with US companies and is expected to advance both US and regional security. The agreement upholds high standards of nuclear safety and nonproliferation. The US energy department will now work with Saudi Arabia to implement the deal.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones have hit two warehouses belonging to Russia's online retailer Wildberries, killing seven workers and injuring dozens, in attacks that have disrupted supplies to the Russian army and frustrated hundreds of Russian sellers. The strikes targeted warehouses in Elektrostal east of Moscow and Kotovsk in the Tambov region, causing estimated damages of £750m. This marks a new phase in Ukraine's attacks on Russia's logistics hubs, used to buy military equipment. The impact on small and medium-sized businesses will be closely watched.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported a negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion for the first time in at least a decade, due to increasing spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company's AI costs are expected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion. This surge in spending comes as major tech companies invest heavily in AI technology. Alphabet's stock fell 4% in after-hours trading following the announcement.

stories.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
stories.summary: South Korean filmmaker Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. The series marks a collaboration between Netflix and Yeon, who developed the project and co-wrote the script with Ryu Yong-jae. This is Yeon's second project with Netflix, following "Hellbound." The series is expected to premiere next year.

stories.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for criticizing a late penalty decision in May. The ban is an immediate three-game suspension with one game suspended until the end of the season. McInnes disputes the decision, saying it was directed at the specific call, not officials in general. The club will now appeal the ruling.

stories.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
stories.summary: Scotland's Marshall and Adlington have been chosen to carry the country's flag at the Commonwealth Games. The honor will be shared by the 59-year-old bowls player and 39-year-old judo athlete, who have been keeping the news secret for weeks. They will lead the Scottish team into the opening ceremony on July 27. The flag will be returned to its rightful owners after the event, but not before a possible battle between the two for the first photo opportunity.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, a 23-year-old who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The deal would reportedly beat out Arsenal and Bayern Munich. Barcola's World Cup performance has sparked interest from multiple top clubs. Liverpool is now in a strong position to secure the signing.

stories.title: ‘Doctor Strange’ Director Says Elon Musk ‘Doesn’t Know S— About Cinema’ After Musk Vows to Make AI ‘Odyssey’: ‘His Opinions on Art Are Worthless’
stories.summary: Elon Musk has vowed to create an AI-generated film adaptation of Homer's "The Odyssey" by the end of the year, sparking controversy in the film industry. Director Scott Derrickson has publicly criticized Musk, stating he "doesn't know shit about cinema" and that his opinions on art are worthless. Musk's AI tool Grok has already generated a three-minute clip of an AI-adapted scene from the poem. The feud between Musk and filmmakers continues, with no clear resolution in sight.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant if attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz continue. The potential targets include those located near or in the capital Tehran. This warning comes after the US launched strikes on Iran for the 11th consecutive night. Iran has activated air defences in Tehran and warned of reprisals.

stories.title: A year after deadly jet crash at Bangladesh school, families demand answers
stories.summary: A year after a Bangladeshi air force jet crashed into a school in Dhaka, killing 36 people, mostly children, families and survivors are still seeking answers. The deadly incident injured over 160 people and left many students struggling to return to normal life. Families of some victims gathered at a hospital in Dhaka to mark the anniversary, demanding justice and closure. The investigation into the crash is ongoing, with many questions still unanswered.

stories.title: Meta won’t have to face the next planned social media addiction trial
stories.summary: A 15-year-old plaintiff from Florida has dropped a lawsuit against Meta, just days before a planned trial in a Los Angeles courtroom. The case, brought by R.K.C., was one of several bellwether trials testing claims that social media companies broke the law by creating addictive features. Meta will not have to face a settlement or trial as a result of the dropped case. The plaintiff's lawyers say he will focus on recovery and therapy instead.

stories.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
stories.summary: A 3-hour film, "The Odyssey," starring Matt Damon, is being screened in 4DX format at select theaters. This immersive experience features seat-rumbling and water-spritzing technology. Over 100 theaters worldwide have adopted this format. Audiences are bracing themselves for a unique cinematic experience.

stories.title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
stories.summary: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

stories.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to $112 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
stories.summary: Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

stories.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Amid Push for Private Security
stories.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report has found that ultra-processed foods are a primary driver of obesity and type 2 diabetes in the country. The report recommends targeting sugar and refined carbohydrates, rather than fat, in food labeling and reformulation efforts. The committee's findings are based on evidence showing that sugar and refined carbohydrates are the main contributors to weight gain. The UK government is now expected to implement new policies to reduce the consumption of these unhealthy ingredients.
